prompt_text: Summarize the passage in one or two concise sentences.
input_text: The city library will begin a phased renovation in September after years of deferred maintenance. The first phase replaces the leaking roof, modernizes the heating system, and upgrades lighting in the reading rooms. A second phase scheduled for winter will improve accessibility by widening aisles, adding an elevator to the west wing, and reconfiguring service desks so visitors using wheelchairs can receive assistance more easily. During construction, the children's room will move to a temporary community center space two blocks away, while the digital media lab will remain open with reduced hours. Project leaders estimate that total costs will remain within the approved budget because donated furniture and volunteer labor will reduce finishing expenses.
target_text: The library renovation starts in September and upgrades maintenance and accessibility while staying within budget.

prompt_text: Summarize the passage in one or two concise sentences.
input_text: Regional planners reviewed flooding outcomes after restoring a network of wetlands along the lower river. Engineers found that the restored marshes slowed runoff during two heavy storms, reducing peak water levels in nearby neighborhoods by several centimeters. Farmers downstream initially worried that standing water would remain on their land longer, but monitoring showed that drainage returned to normal within three days. The report also notes that bird populations increased during the first spring after restoration and that maintenance costs were lower than expected because native vegetation outcompeted most invasive species. Officials now recommend expanding the project to two additional tributaries before the next rainy season.
target_text: Restored wetlands reduced flooding, recovered drainage quickly, and are now recommended for expansion.

prompt_text: Summarize the passage in one or two concise sentences.
input_text: A regional hospital tested a discharge pilot for older patients with chronic conditions. Under the pilot, each patient received a medication review, a printed recovery checklist, and a follow-up phone call within forty-eight hours of leaving the hospital. Nurses reported that the calls often identified confusion about dosage timing or missed appointments before those issues became serious. Over the three-month trial, readmissions fell modestly, but staff emphasized that the strongest benefit was improved confidence among patients and family caregivers. Administrators are now deciding whether to extend the program permanently and whether translators should be added for patients who prefer languages other than Korean or English.
target_text: The discharge pilot improved follow-up support, raised patient confidence, and slightly reduced readmissions.

prompt_text: Summarize the passage in one or two concise sentences.
input_text: A district tutoring program paired middle-school students with university mentors for twelve weeks. Sessions focused on reading comprehension, note-taking, and planning longer assignments rather than drilling standardized test questions. Teachers observed that students who attended regularly were more willing to ask questions in class and submitted fewer incomplete essays by the end of the term. Attendance was strongest when tutoring took place immediately after school and when guardians received weekly progress messages. The evaluation warns, however, that gains may fade if the district cannot keep mentor turnover low or if transportation remains unreliable for students who live farther from campus.
target_text: The tutoring program improved engagement and assignment completion, though stable staffing and transportation remain important.

prompt_text: Choose the single best topic label and output only that label.
input_text: A district tutoring program paired middle-school students with university mentors for twelve weeks. Sessions focused on reading comprehension, note-taking, and planning longer assignments rather than drilling standardized test questions. Teachers observed that students who attended regularly were more willing to ask questions in class and submitted fewer incomplete essays by the end of the term. Attendance was strongest when tutoring took place immediately after school and when guardians received weekly progress messages. The evaluation warns, however, that gains may fade if the district cannot keep mentor turnover low or if transportation remains unreliable for students who live farther from campus.

prompt_text: Summarize the passage in one or two concise sentences.
input_text: A housing cooperative installed rooftop solar panels on three apartment buildings and tracked electricity use for six months. Residents saw lower common-area energy bills, but savings varied because one building still had outdated refrigeration units in a shared storage room. The trial also revealed that regular cleaning mattered: panels exposed to dust from a nearby construction site produced less power until the cooperative added monthly inspections. Despite those differences, the board concluded that payback would likely arrive sooner than expected if maintenance schedules remained consistent and if a planned battery purchase reduced evening demand charges.
target_text: The solar trial lowered shared energy costs, and maintenance plus battery support could improve savings further.
